title: Bhikkhus, the multitude is immersed in ignorance, blind, covered and fettered. When the Dhamma that subdues ignorance is taught by the Tathāgata, they listen well,…
content: Bhikkhus, the multitude is immersed in ignorance, blind, covered and fettered. When the Dhamma that subdues ignorance is taught by the Tathāgata, they listen well, lend an ear, and apply their minds with right understanding. This, bhikkhus, is the fourth wonderful, marvelous quality that appears when the Tathāgata, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One appears.

title: And, Ānanda, how is a bhikkhu an island unto himself, a refuge unto himself, with no other refuge, having the Dhamma as his island, the…
content: And, Ānanda, how is a bhikkhu an island unto himself, a refuge unto himself, with no other refuge, having the Dhamma as his island, the Dhamma as his refuge, with no other refuge? Ānanda, here a bhikkhu dwells contemplating the body as a body, ardent, clearly comprehending, mindful, having removed covetousness and displeasure in regard to the world; he dwells contemplating feelings as feelings ... mind as mind ... phenomena as phenomena, ardent, clearly comprehending, mindful, having removed covetousness and displeasure in regard to the world. Ānanda, that is how a bhikkhu is an island unto himself, a refuge unto himself, with no other refuge, having the Dhamma as his island, the Dhamma as his refuge, with no other refuge. Ānanda, those who now or after my passing are islands unto themselves, refuges unto themselves, with no other refuge, having the Dhamma as their island, the Dhamma as their refuge, with no other refuge — these, Ānanda, will be the foremost among my bhikkhus, if they are eager to learn.

title: Elder Nun Sumedhā (continued): What is this foul body, Stinking, causing fear, A bag of dead flesh, Oozing impure fluids? What is this body I…
content:
Elder Nun Sumedhā (continued):

What is this foul body,
Stinking, causing fear,
A bag of dead flesh,
Oozing impure fluids?

What is this body I know,
Disgusting, covered with blood and flesh,
A dwelling for worms,
Food for birds?
This body is thus —
To whom would I give it now?

The body, left in a charnel ground,
Before long, without consciousness,
Like a discarded log,
Despised even by relatives.

That body, thrown in a charnel ground,
Becomes food for others.
Formerly they washed it clean,
Now they are disgusted by it.
Even parents are thus —
What to say of the crowd?

They are infatuated with a body without core,
Bound by sinews and bones,
Full of saliva, full of tears,
Full of foul excrement and urine.

If the body were cut open,
Turning the inside out,
Even the mother would be disgusted,
Unable to bear the stench.

It is through aggregates, sense spheres, and elements
That these bodies are formed,
The root of renewed existence, of suffering.
They rightly say
They have no delight at all.
Therefore, whom would I want to marry?

Every day, a hundred spears
Always stabbing the body anew,
Even if harmed for a hundred years —
That would still be better,
For that suffering would finally end.

The wise person would consent
To such killing as described above.
The Teacher taught that
They wander for a long time,
With a mind that kills,
Continuously, one after another.

In the realms of devas and humans,
Animals, asuras,
Ghosts, and hells,
They suffer countless killings.

In hell, many
With defiled minds, fallen,
Find no refuge in heaven,
No bliss of Nibbāna.

Those who attain Nibbāna
Are intimately connected
With the teachings
Of the One with Ten Powers,
Living at ease, not restless,
Striving to end birth and death.

Dear father, today
I am determined to leave.
Those empty possessions
Have no value at all.
I am disgusted with sensual desires,
I am weary of sensual desires.
They have been cut apart
Like a tāla palm trunk.

title: Inner and outer fetters, Beings are fettered by them. I ask Gotama, Who can be freed from this fetter?" ( Blessed One ) One with…
content:
Inner and outer fetters,
Beings are fettered by them.
I ask Gotama,
Who can be freed from this fetter?"

( Blessed One )
One with wisdom, established in virtue,
Developing mind and wisdom,
Ardent and heedful,
That bhikkhu is freed from the fetter.
One who has abandoned
Greed, hatred, and ignorance,
The taint-free Arahant,
That one is freed from the fetter.
Where name-and-form
Is completely destroyed without remainder,
Where the impediment of form-perception is removed,
There the fetter is cut.

title: Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, even if one hears the good Dhamma, one cannot enter the fixed course of rightness in wholesome states. What six? He…
content: Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, even if one hears the good Dhamma, one cannot enter the fixed course of rightness in wholesome states. What six? He is possessed of kamma-obstruction, defilement-obstruction, result-obstruction; he has no faith, no desire (to listen), and he has evil wisdom.

title: And, Ānanda, what is the path, what is the way leading to the abandoning of the five lower fetters? Here, Ānanda, a bhikkhu, through the…
content: And, Ānanda, what is the path, what is the way leading to the abandoning of the five lower fetters? Here, Ānanda, a bhikkhu, through the seclusion from all bases (upadhiviveka), through the abandoning of unwholesome states, through the complete calming down of all bodily coarseness, secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, enters and abides in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by initial and sustained thought, with rapture and happiness born of seclusion.

title: Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time five things do not occur for him: Suffering…
content: Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time five things do not occur for him: Suffering and grief connected with sensual pleasures do not occur for him at that time. Pleasure and joy connected with sensual pleasures do not occur for him at that time. Suffering and grief connected with the unwholesome do not occur for him at that time. Pleasure and joy connected with the unwholesome do not occur for him at that time. Suffering and grief connected with the wholesome do not occur for him at that time. Sāriputta, while a noble disciple attains and abides in the joy born of seclusion, at that time these five things do not occur for him.

title: One who lives without fever, At death does not grieve. If the wise person sees the path, Among the grieving, does not grieve. A bhikkhu…
content:
One who lives without fever,
At death does not grieve.
If the wise person sees the path,
Among the grieving, does not grieve.

A bhikkhu who has cut off craving for existence,
With mind at peace,
The wandering in birth and death is ended,
That one is not reborn.

title: Venerable Mettagū: I ask the Blessed One, may the Blessed One answer me, The question I have asked — I think the Lord is wise,…
content:
Venerable Mettagū:
I ask the Blessed One, may the Blessed One answer me,
The question I have asked — I think the Lord is wise,
Self-developed. From what in this world does this suffering arise,
With so many kinds?

The Blessed One replied to Mettagū:
You ask me about the origin of suffering;
I will tell you as I have known:
From a basis, suffering arises, with many kinds,
Various in the world.

Those who, because of ignorance, create a basis,
That fool repeatedly encounters suffering.
Therefore, a wise person should not create a basis,
Seeing the origin of birth and suffering.

title: The unreal they take for real, The real they take for unreal. They never reach the real, By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct. Having…
content:
The unreal they take for real,
The real they take for unreal.
They never reach the real,
By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct.

Having known the real as real,
And the unreal as unreal,
They reach the real,
By their right thoughts and right conduct.
